title: Building Secure APIs with JWT Authentication
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Protecting Your APIs with JSON Web Tokens
paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to implement secure authentication in your APIs using JSON Web Tokens (JWT). From generating tokens to verifying signatures, we'll cover everything you need to know to protect your APIs from unauthorized access.

point_description.title: JWT Basics
points: Understanding the structure of JSON Web Tokens
Generating JWT tokens with a secret key
Verifying JWT tokens with the same secret key
Signing and encrypting JWT tokens for added security
Configuring token expiration and refresh
Storing JWT tokens securely on the client side

point_description.title: Implementing JWT Authentication
points: Adding JWT authentication middleware to your API routes
Verifying JWT tokens and extracting payload data
Protecting routes and resources with authentication middleware
Handling token expiration and refreshing tokens
Revoking JWT tokens to invalidate sessions
Logging in and out users with JWT

title: Building Real-Time Chat Applications with Socket.IO
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build real-time chat applications with Socket.IO, a JavaScript library for real-time web applications. From basic chat rooms to advanced features like private messaging, we'll cover everything you need to know to create engaging chat experiences.

point_description.title: Getting Started with Socket.IO
points: Setting up a Socket.IO server with Node.js
Configuring WebSocket connections for real-time communication
Handling connection events and broadcasting messages
Joining chat rooms and managing user sessions
Implementing message history and persistence
Scaling Socket.IO applications for high concurrency

point_description.title: Building Chat Features
points: Creating real-time chat rooms with Socket.IO
Implementing private messaging and user authentication
Adding presence indicators and online status
Integrating chat into existing web applications
Optimizing chat performance and reliability
Testing real-time features with Socket.IO

title: Building Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) with React
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Creating Fast, Reliable, and Engaging Web Experiences
paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) with React, combining the power of web technologies with the capabilities of native apps. From offline support to push notifications, we'll cover everything you need to know to create modern web experiences.

point_description.title: Introduction to PWAs
points: Understanding the principles of Progressive Web Apps
Key features: offline support, push notifications, installability
Advantages of PWAs over traditional web and native apps
PWA requirements and best practices
Browser support and compatibility
Building blocks of PWAs: service workers, web app manifests

point_description.title: Building PWAs with React
points: Creating a responsive and mobile-first layout with React
Implementing service workers for offline support
Configuring web app manifests for installability
Adding push notifications with the Notifications API
Caching assets and data for improved performance
Testing and debugging PWAs across different devices

title: Optimizing GraphQL APIs for Mobile Clients
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Delivering Efficient Data to Mobile Apps
paragraph_description.paragraph: Explore strategies for optimizing GraphQL APIs to meet the unique requirements of mobile clients. From reducing payload sizes to minimizing network requests, we'll cover techniques to ensure your GraphQL APIs perform well on mobile devices.

point_description.title: Reducing Payload Sizes
points: Minimizing field selections and nesting in GraphQL queries
Using fragments and aliases for efficient data fetching
Implementing pagination and cursor-based navigation
Compressing JSON payloads with gzip or Brotli
Optimizing image and media delivery for mobile devices
Using persisted queries for caching and precompilation

point_description.title: Minimizing Network Requests
points: Combining multiple queries with batched requests
Implementing data stitching and federation for microservices
Using persisted queries for reduced round trips
Caching and prefetching data on the client side
Using CDN caching for static assets and responses
Monitoring and profiling network performance

title: Building GraphQL APIs with Apollo Server
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Creating Scalable and Performant GraphQL APIs
paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build GraphQL APIs with Apollo Server, the popular GraphQL server library for Node.js. From schema design to data fetching, we'll cover everything you need to know to create production-ready GraphQL APIs with Apollo Server.

point_description.title: Setting Up Apollo Server
points: Initializing a new Apollo Server project with npm or yarn
Defining a GraphQL schema with Apollo Server
Implementing resolver functions for schema fields
Fetching data from external APIs or databases
Configuring Apollo Server with data sources and plugins
Integrating Apollo Server with existing Node.js applications

point_description.title: Optimizing GraphQL Performance
points: Implementing data loaders for efficient data fetching
Using persisted queries for caching and precompilation
Reducing resolver complexity with schema stitching and delegation
Implementing response caching with Apollo Server plugins
Monitoring and profiling GraphQL query performance
Scaling Apollo Server for high traffic applications

title: Automated Testing Strategies for React Applications
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Discover automated testing strategies and best practices for React applications. From unit tests to end-to-end testing, we'll cover a variety of techniques to ensure your React code is reliable, maintainable, and bug-free.

point_description.title: Introduction to Automated Testing
points: Understanding the benefits of automated testing
Different types of automated tests: unit, integration, end-to-end
Testing pyramid and the balance between different test types
Tools and libraries for automated testing in React applications
Setting up testing environments with Jest and React Testing Library
Writing testable code for easier testing

point_description.title: Writing and Running Tests
points: Writing unit tests with Jest and testing React components
Mocking dependencies for isolated unit tests
Testing React hooks and custom hooks
Writing integration tests for complex interactions
End-to-end testing with tools like Cypress or Selenium
Running tests locally and in continuous integration

title: Implementing Authentication and Authorization in Next.js
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to implement authentication and authorization in your Next.js applications to protect routes and resources from unauthorized access. From session management to role-based permissions, we'll cover everything you need to know to build secure Next.js apps.

point_description.title: Session-Based Authentication
points: Managing user sessions with cookies and server-side storage
Implementing session authentication middleware
Protecting routes and resources with session validation
Storing session data securely and managing expiration
Logging in and logging out users with session tokens
Remembering users with persistent sessions

point_description.title: Role-Based Authorization
points: Defining user roles and permissions
Restricting access to routes and resources based on roles
Implementing role-based authorization middleware
Handling unauthorized access and redirecting users
Managing user roles and permissions dynamically
Auditing user activity and access logs

title: Building Real-Time Dashboards with React and Chart.js
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build real-time dashboards with React and Chart.js, a JavaScript library for data visualization. From live data streaming to interactive charts, we'll cover everything you need to know to create dynamic and responsive dashboards.

point_description.title: Getting Started with Chart.js
points: Installing Chart.js and setting up a basic chart
Configuring chart options and styling
Creating different types of charts: line, bar, pie, etc.
Binding data to charts and updating in real time
Customizing tooltips, legends, and labels
Handling user interactions with interactive charts

point_description.title: Building Real-Time Dashboards
points: Connecting React components to real-time data sources
Streaming live data to the client with WebSocket or SSE
Updating charts in real time with new data
Implementing data aggregation and filtering
Optimizing dashboard performance for large datasets
Adding additional features like date range pickers and data exports

title: Building RESTful APIs with Express.js
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build RESTful APIs with Express.js, the popular web framework for Node.js. From routing to middleware, we'll cover everything you need to know to create production-ready APIs with Express.

point_description.title: Setting Up Express.js
points: Initializing a new Express.js project with npm or yarn
Creating routes for different API endpoints
Handling HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
Parsing request bodies and handling query parameters
Implementing middleware for authentication, validation, etc.
Error handling and response formatting

point_description.title: Working with Databases and Models
points: Connecting Express.js to databases like MongoDB or PostgreSQL
Defining data models with Mongoose or Sequelize
CRUD operations with database models
Implementing relationships between models
Handling database migrations and schema changes
Optimizing database queries for performance

title: Building Multiplayer Games with WebSocket and Socket.IO
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build multiplayer games with WebSocket and Socket.IO, enabling real-time communication between players and servers. From game mechanics to network protocols, we'll cover everything you need to know to create engaging multiplayer experiences.

point_description.title: Understanding Real-Time Game Development
points: Challenges and considerations for real-time multiplayer games
Networking protocols for game communication: TCP, UDP, WebSockets
Server architectures for handling game logic and player interactions
Latency compensation and synchronization techniques
Security considerations for multiplayer game servers
Scalability and performance optimization

point_description.title: Building Multiplayer Games with WebSocket
points: Setting up WebSocket server with Node.js and ws library
Handling connection events and managing game sessions
Broadcasting game state to connected clients
Implementing player movement and interactions
Synchronizing game state across multiple clients
Optimizing network performance for real-time gaming

title: Building Scalable Web Scrapers with Node.js
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build web scrapers with Node.js to extract data from websites and APIs. From basic scraping techniques to advanced crawling strategies, we'll cover everything you need to know to build scalable and efficient web scrapers.

point_description.title: Introduction to Web Scraping
points: Overview of web scraping and its use cases
Understanding HTML structure and DOM traversal
Basic scraping techniques with libraries like Cheerio
Fetching and parsing HTML content with Node.js
Handling dynamic content with JavaScript rendering
Respecting robots.txt and website terms of service

point_description.title: Building Web Scrapers with Node.js
points: Implementing web scrapers with Axios and Cheerio
Configuring scraping pipelines and data extraction
Handling pagination and dynamic content loading
Dealing with anti-scraping techniques like rate limiting and IP blocking
Persisting scraped data to databases or files
Scaling scrapers for large-scale data extraction

title: Building Real-Time Collaborative Editing Tools with Yjs
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Enabling Real-Time Collaboration in Web Applications
paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build real-time collaborative editing tools with Yjs, a JavaScript library for building collaborative applications. From text editors to whiteboards, we'll cover everything you need to know to create collaborative experiences in your web apps.

point_description.title: Getting Started with Yjs
points: Installing Yjs and integrating it into your web application
Configuring shared data structures with Yjs
Implementing collaborative text editing with Y.Text
Handling user cursors and selections in real time
Synchronizing shared data across multiple clients
Conflict resolution and operational transforms

point_description.title: Building Collaborative Editing Tools
points: Creating real-time text editors with collaborative features
Implementing presence indicators and user avatars
Building collaborative drawing and whiteboard applications
Adding chat and commenting features to collaborative tools
Optimizing performance and scalability for large user bases
Testing collaborative features with multiple users

title: Building Microservices with Node.js and Docker
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build microservices with Node.js and Docker to create scalable and maintainable distributed systems. From service architecture to containerization, we'll cover everything you need to know to design, develop, and deploy microservices.

point_description.title: Introduction to Microservices
points: Understanding the principles of microservices architecture
Advantages of microservices over monolithic architectures
Decomposing applications into smaller, independent services
Communication patterns between microservices: REST, gRPC, etc.
Service discovery and orchestration with tools like Kubernetes
Challenges and considerations for microservices

point_description.title: Building Microservices with Node.js and Docker
points: Creating microservices with Express.js and other Node.js frameworks
Containerizing microservices with Docker and Docker Compose
Defining service interfaces and APIs
Implementing inter-service communication with HTTP or messaging queues
Scaling microservices horizontally and vertically
Monitoring and logging for microservices

title: Building Real-Time Auction Applications with Firebase
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.paragraph: Learn how to build real-time auction applications with Firebase, Google's platform for building mobile and web applications. From real-time bidding to auction management, we'll cover everything you need to know to create engaging auction experiences.

point_description.title: Setting Up Firebase for Auctions
points: Creating a new Firebase project in the Firebase Console
Configuring Firebase Authentication for user management
Setting up Firebase Realtime Database for real-time data synchronization
Implementing Firebase Cloud Functions for serverless backend logic
Deploying auction web app with Firebase Hosting
Securing Firebase resources with Firebase Security Rules

point_description.title: Building Auction Features
points: Creating real-time bidding interfaces with Firebase Realtime Database
Implementing auction countdowns and status updates
Handling bid validation and auction rules
Notifying users of auction events with Firebase Cloud Messaging
Implementing user authentication and authorization
Monitoring auction activity and analytics

title: Introduction to Deno: A Secure Runtime for JavaScript
author: Khandoker Shamimul Haque

paragraph_description.header: Exploring the Next Generation JavaScript Runtime
paragraph_description.paragraph: Discover Deno, a secure runtime for JavaScript and TypeScript built on the V8 JavaScript engine and Rust. From module system to security features, we'll cover everything you need to know to get started with Deno.

point_description.title: Understanding Deno
points: Introduction to Deno and its creator, Ryan Dahl
Key differences between Deno and Node.js
Secure by default: Deno's security model and permissions
Module system and import statements in Deno
Built-in utilities and standard library in Deno
Compatibility with existing Node.js modules

point_description.title: Getting Started with Deno
points: Installing Deno on your system with Shell or Package Manager
Running Deno scripts and applications
Managing dependencies with import statements
Using Deno runtime flags for configuration
Building and distributing Deno applications
Exploring Deno's standard library and third-party modules
